human_verification.notes: Reviewed the construction-and-field-services customer-project-communication package for boundary quality, authority-source quality, complaint and exception handling, permit and inspection gating, closeout dependency coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to construction and field-services customer project communication with permit, inspection, safety, closeout, complaint, and authority-aware status handling.

Authority sources cover consumer-contractor communication, complaint evidence, permit-holder duties, final-inspection prerequisites, OSHA safety stop conditions, closeout documentation, and public construction progress-report standards with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover policy exceptions, missing prerequisites, permit or site-readiness gaps, inspection or closeout blockers, trade-authority refusal, changed safety conditions, repeat-failure escalation, terminology handling, and tenant adaptation.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/construction-and-field-services/customer-project-communication-specialist/ to keep this overlay in the canonical NAICS-derived path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around permit approval, inspection signoff, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, compensation approval, and legal interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated construction and field-services customer project communication overlay where legal, permit, inspection, safety, engineering, and licensed-trade authority remain external.
The specialist is intentionally communication-oriented: it knows when to hand work to project controls, permitting, inspection, superintendent, safety, change-order, legal, and licensed-trade lanes.
